evidence_text: The study collected and analyzed 328 Claude Code configuration files from public projects.
source_excerpt: "This paper presents an empirical study of the configuration ecosystem of Claude Code... We collected and analyzed 328 configuration files from public Claude Code projects"
note: The paper describes these as publicly available Claude Code project configuration files.
caveat: The paper describes these as publicly available Claude Code project configuration files.

evidence_text: The initial GitHub Search API query found 4,724 repositories containing a Claude.md file between August 28 and August 30, 2025.
source_excerpt: "We first collected an initial list of GitHub repositories which are using Claude Code between August 28, 2025 and August 30, 2025... This initial search returned 4,724 repositories."
note: This is the pre-filter search result, not the final study sample.
caveat: This is the pre-filter search result, not the final study sample.

evidence_text: The final repository sample was the top 100 most popular repositories remaining after filtering for popularity, English language, and real-world applications.
source_excerpt: "We removed repositories based on the following criteria: (1) Popularity... less than 100 stars... (2) Language... non-English repositories... (3) Actual Applications... not real-world applications... From the remaining ones, we selected the top-100 most popular repositories"
note: The paper states these filters were applied before fetching files from the selected repositories.
caveat: The paper states these filters were applied before fetching files from the selected repositories.

evidence_text: The selected projects span 23 programming languages, with JavaScript/TypeScript in 35 projects, Python in 16, and Go in 9.
source_excerpt: "Overall, the projects are implemented in 23 programming languages, with JavaScript/TypeScript (35 projects), Python (16 projects), and Go (9 projects) being the most common ones."
note: This describes language distribution within the selected repository sample.
caveat: This describes language distribution within the selected repository sample.

evidence_text: The selected projects had a median of 950 stars, 488 commits, and 58 months of age since first commit.
citation: Santos et al., popularity and activity medians
source_excerpt: "All projects are fairly popular (median of 950 stars) and active (median of 488 commits). They are also long-lived, with a median age of 58 months since their first commit."
note: These are medians reported for the selected repositories, not for individual configuration files.
caveat: These are medians reported for the selected repositories, not for individual configuration files.

evidence_text: During file fetching, the authors found 45 CLAUDE.md files that acted as memory banks pointing to other markdown files, and they retrieved the referenced files instead.
source_excerpt: "During this process, we detected 45 files functioning as memory banks, i.e., files that only pointed to other markdown files within their respective projects. In such cases, we retrieved the contents of the referenced files instead."
note: This handling step affects how the final 328-file dataset was assembled.
caveat: This handling step affects how the final 328-file dataset was assembled.

evidence_text: The manual analysis focused on level-2 markdown headings, except for 36 files with no level-2 sections, where level-1 headings were analyzed instead.
source_excerpt: "To answer the proposed RQs, we focused on level 2 sections... However, there is also one file with 213 headings. Interestingly, we found that 36 files have no level-2 sections at all... in such cases, we decided to analyze the content of level-1 headings instead."
note: The paper uses markdown section structure as the unit for identifying concerns and practices.
caveat: The paper uses markdown section structure as the unit for identifying concerns and practices.

evidence_text: The configuration files had a median of 7 level-2 headings, with a maximum of 213 and 36 files with zero level-2 headings.
source_excerpt: "Table 1 shows the distribution of the number of level-2 headings in the dataset... the files have a median of seven level-2 headings. However, there is also one file with 213 headings... we found that 36 files have no level-2 sections at all."
note: The reported size measure is number of ## sections.
caveat: The reported size measure is number of ## sections.

evidence_text: The authors extracted 2,492 section titles and manually grouped them into semantically related categories, with one author performing the initial grouping and two others confirming it.
citation: Santos et al., manual classification process
source_excerpt: "After following the previous procedures, we extracted 2,492 section titles. These sections were then manually analyzed by one of the authors, who grouped them into semantically related categories... Next, a meeting was held with two other authors, who inspected the proposed classification and confirmed it."
note: The replication package reportedly contains the original section titles, proposed classification, and links to the source files.
caveat: The replication package reportedly contains the original section titles, proposed classification, and links to the source files.

evidence_text: The co-occurrence analysis used the FP-Max algorithm from MLxtend with minimum support set to 0.15.
source_excerpt: "We used the FP-Max algorithm to detect which concerns and practices are mentioned together... We used the FP-Max implementation provided by the MLxtend library, configured with 0.15 minimum support."
note: Each Claude.md file was represented as a transaction containing the concerns and practices it included.
caveat: Each Claude.md file was represented as a transaction containing the concerns and practices it included.

evidence_text: Architecture was the most common concern category, appearing in 238 Claude.md files, or 72.6% of the dataset.
citation: Santos et al., most common concern
source_excerpt: "these concerns and practices are related to the architecture of the software to be generated with the support of AI agents (found in 238 Claude.md files from our dataset, 72.6%)"
note: This is the highest-frequency category reported in the RQ1 results.
caveat: This is the highest-frequency category reported in the RQ1 results.

evidence_text: Development guidelines appeared in 147 files (44.8%), project overview in 128 (39%), testing in 116 (35.4%), and commands in 109 (33.2%).
citation: Santos et al., other common concerns
source_excerpt: "followed by general development guidelines (147 Claude.md files, 44.8%), project overview (128 Claude.md files, 39%), and testing guidelines (116 Claude.md files, 35.4%) and commands (109 Claude.md files, 33.2%)."
note: These are the next most frequent categories reported after architecture.
caveat: These are the next most frequent categories reported after architecture.

evidence_text: Code examples were most common in Development Guidelines sections at 17.68%, while links were most common in Architecture sections at 1.83%; the authors found only two Mermaid UML diagram instances.
citation: Santos et al., code examples and links
source_excerpt: "As shown, the highest percentage of code examples appears in the Development Guidelines category (17.68% of the instances classified in this category)... Conversely, links were most frequently found in the Architecture category, though in only 1.83% of the files... we also searched for diagrams... but found only two instances."
note: These percentages are reported by category in the RQ2 analysis.
caveat: These percentages are reported by category in the RQ2 analysis.

evidence_text: The most common configuration pattern was Architecture + Dependencies + Project Overview at 21.6% of files, but the classification underlying these results was initially performed by one author.
citation: Santos et al., top pattern and validity caveat
source_excerpt: "This paper presents an empirical study of the configuration ecosystem of Claude Code... We collected and analyzed 328 configuration files from public Claude Code projects"
note: The paper describes these as publicly available Claude Code project configuration files.
caveat: The paper describes these as publicly available Claude Code project configuration files.
